full_title: Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors and Immune-Related Adverse Events in Patients With Advanced Melanoma: A Systematic Review and Network Meta-analysis

search.pubmed_query: (("immune checkpoint inhibitors"[Title/Abstract] OR "checkpoint inhibitor"[Title/Abstract] OR "ipilimumab"[MeSH Terms] OR "ipilimumab"[Title/Abstract] OR "nivolumab"[MeSH Terms] OR "nivolumab"[Title/Abstract] OR "pembrolizumab"[MeSH Terms] OR "pembrolizumab"[Title/Abstract] OR "anti-PD-1"[Title/Abstract] OR "anti-PD-L1"[Title/Abstract] OR "anti-CTLA-4"[Title/Abstract]) AND ("melanoma"[MeSH Terms] OR "melanoma"[Title/Abstract] OR "advanced melanoma"[Title/Abstract] OR "metastatic melanoma"[Title/Abstract]) AND ("Randomized Controlled Trial"[Publication Type] OR "randomized"[Title/Abstract] OR "randomised"[Title/Abstract] OR "phase II"[Title/Abstract] OR "phase III"[Title/Abstract])) AND ("2010/01/01"[PDAT] : "2019/06/30"[PDAT])
search.notes: Reconstructed from Chang 2020 eTable 1 (supplement). Original search used controlled vocabulary (MeSH, Emtree) and text words developed with a research librarian. Also searched Embase, Web of Science, Scopus. This PubMed query is a conservative reproduction.

inclusion_criteria.study_design: Randomized controlled trial (phase II or III)
inclusion_criteria.population: Patients with advanced (unresectable or metastatic) melanoma
inclusion_criteria.intervention: Immune checkpoint inhibitor (ipilimumab, nivolumab, pembrolizumab) alone or in combination

exclusion_criteria: Observational studies, case reports, case series
Studies not reporting ICI as primary intervention
